mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals a structural labor market cooling that reduces consumer spending capacity, forcing equity markets to price in lower corporate earnings over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, which directly reduces revenue expectations and drives negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail and housing data yield positive 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.0 percent month-over-month during supply-constrained periods signals capacity utilization bottlenecks, triggering inflation expectations and immediate short-term equity drawdowns.
failure_condition: High industrial production growth correlates with positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases during the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation runs above 3.0 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 3.0 percent, real interest rates turn deeply negative, fueling speculative equity positioning and positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Real rate compression fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and wage growth, supporting corporate revenue projections and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 60-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent signals economic deceleration, prompting immediate risk-off positioning by institutional investors and generating negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: GDP deceleration triggers positive 5-day returns in more than 70 percent of instances during the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates a synchronized economic expansion, boosting earnings expectations and generating positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields negative returns in more than 60 percent of cases over a 3-year walk-forward test.
